mission.title: vault: Vault UI Feature Request: Don't Show all login methods in Vault UI Login Page
mission.description: The Vault UI login page shows all configured authentication methods in the dropdown, including unconfigured ones. This confuses users who see methods they should not use. The recommended workaround is to use the listing_visibility setting to control which auth methods appear. Requested by 99+ users.

steps.title: Check current Vault setup
steps.description:
Verify your Vault version and list enabled auth methods:
```bash
vault version
vault auth list -detailed
```
Note which auth methods are enabled and which should be hidden from the UI login page.

steps.title: Understand the listing_visibility setting
steps.description:
Vault provides a `listing_visibility` tune parameter that controls whether an auth method appears in the UI login dropdown. By default, all enabled auth methods are listed. Setting `listing_visibility` to `unauth` makes the method visible to unauthenticated users (default UI behavior), while omitting it hides the method from the dropdown.

You can also query the internal UI mounts API to see what is currently visible:
```bash
vault read sys/internal/ui/mounts
```

steps.title: Configure listing_visibility for auth methods
steps.description:
To hide an auth method from the UI login dropdown, tune its listing_visibility:
```bash
# Show a specific auth method in the UI login dropdown:
vault auth tune -listing-visibility=unauth userpass/

# To hide it again (remove from dropdown), set to empty:
vault auth tune -listing-visibility='' token/
```
Only auth methods with `listing_visibility=unauth` will appear in the UI login page dropdown. This lets you present only the methods your users should actually use.

steps.title: Verify the UI login page shows only desired methods
steps.description:
Open the Vault UI login page and confirm:
1. Only the auth methods you configured with `listing_visibility=unauth` appear in the dropdown
2. Unconfigured or internal-only methods are no longer shown
3. Users can still authenticate with the expected methods

resolution.summary: Vault's listing_visibility auth tune parameter controls which authentication methods appear in the UI login dropdown. Setting listing_visibility=unauth on an auth method makes it visible on the login page, while methods without this setting are hidden. This prevents user confusion from seeing unconfigured or internal-only auth methods.

prerequisites.description: A running Vault server with admin access to tune auth methods.
